insight: In the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Canada's performance during hydration breaks has been notable, with the team gaining momentum in both of their breaks so far, resulting in a mean increase of 0.150 points per minute. This momentum gain has been consistent, particularly when Canada was pressing before the break, as they maintained their momentum in both instances, suggesting that the breaks have allowed them to regroup and refocus their efforts. The fact that Canada has only pressed before breaks twice and has not had any instances where they were not pressing, means that there is limited data on how they perform in other scenarios, but the existing data indicates a positive impact from the hydration breaks on their overall performance. Overall, the hydration breaks seem to be giving Canada a slight edge, allowing them to come back stronger and more focused, which could be a crucial factor in their matches.
